Course codes extracted: none Detected section role: admission-prep-section Schedulable: yes Reason: inherits nearby source-section role Issue: none

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Here at UW Bothell, we think of STS as being the study of how science and technology are made, and how they form part of social and political life. For us, STS stands for “Science, Technology and Society,” but it can also mean “Science and Technology Studies,” or even “Social Studies of Science and Technology.”
sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: STS majors take courses that examine science and technology from a broad range of perspectives. In Understanding Statistics (BIS 315) and Science Methods and Practice (BES 301), students learn to think like a scientist – to formulate research hypotheses, collect data, and reason with numbers. In Science, Technology, and Society (BISSTS 307), the core class in the STS major, students learn to think like a social scientist, and to approach science as a social, cultural, and political practice. For their remaining STS courses, students choose classes that examine how social, scientific, and technological factors intertwine in areas ranging from environmental restoration to global health.

For us, STS stands for “Science, Technology and Society,” but it can also mean “Science and Technology Studies,” or even “Social Studies of Science and Technology.” Raw line: STS majors take courses that examine science and technology from a broad range of perspectives. In Science, Technology, and Society (BISSTS 307), the core class in the STS major, students learn to think like a social scientist, and to approach science as a social, cultural, and political practice. sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Here at UW Bothell, we think of STS as being the study of how science and technology are made, and how they form part of social and political life. For us, STS stands for “Science, Technology and Society,” but it can also mean “Science and Technology Studies,” or even “Social Studies of Science and Technology.”
sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: STS is a major field of study within the Interdisciplinary Arts and Sciences (IAS) Program. STS majors primarily take courses offered through the IAS program, including Interdisciplinary Inquiry (BIS 300) and the Portfolio Capstone (BIS 499). S&T refers to the Science and Technology Program, which has its own majors (like Biology and Electrical Engineering) and an independent set of degree requirements. Students in the STS program may take S&T classes to fulfill General Elective and Natural World requirements – just as S&T students may take STS courses to fulfill some of the requirements for their majors.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Yes! Many U.S. universities (not to mention a larger number of European ones) offer master’s degrees and doctorates in Science and Technology Studies or closely related fields like History and Philosophy of Science. But STS majors are also well equipped for graduate programs in the social sciences and humanities, including anthropology, sociology, history, and policy studies, as well as for law school and M.B.A. programs.

[parser prerequisite filter audit] Owner id: uw-bothell-science-technology-and-society Source URL: https://www.uwb.edu/ias/undergraduate/majors/science-technology-society Section title: Here at UW Bothell, we think of STS as being the study of how science and technology are made, and how they form part of social and political life. For us, STS stands for “Science, Technology and Society,” but it can also mean “Science and Technology Studies,” or even “Social Studies of Science and Technology.” Raw line: STS majors take courses that examine science and technology from a broad range of perspectives. In Understanding Statistics (BIS 315) and Science Methods and Practice (BES 301), students learn to think like a scientist – to formulate research hypotheses, collect data, and reason with numbers. In Science, Technology, and Society (BISSTS 307), the core class in the STS major, students learn to think like a social scientist, and to approach science as a social, cultural, and political practice. For their remaining STS courses, students choose classes that examine how social, scientific, and technological factors intertwine in areas ranging from environmental restoration to global health. Course codes extracted: BES 301, BIS 315, BISSTS 307 Detected section role: primary-requirement-section Schedulable: yes Reason: course row without prerequisite-only or course-list section cues Issue: none

[parser prerequisite filter audit] Owner id: uw-bothell-science-technology-and-society Source URL: https://www.uwb.edu/ias/undergraduate/majors/science-technology-society Section title: Here at UW Bothell, we think of STS as being the study of how science and technology are made, and how they form part of social and political life. For us, STS stands for “Science, Technology and Society,” but it can also mean “Science and Technology Studies,” or even “Social Studies of Science and Technology.” Raw line: STS is a major field of study within the Interdisciplinary Arts and Sciences (IAS) Program. STS majors primarily take courses offered through the IAS program, including Interdisciplinary Inquiry (BIS 300) and the Portfolio Capstone (BIS 499). S&T refers to the Science and Technology Program, which has its own majors (like Biology and Electrical Engineering) and an independent set of degree requirements. Students in the STS program may take S&T classes to fulfill General Elective and Natural World requirements – just as S&T students may take STS courses to fulfill some of the requirements for their majors. Course codes extracted: BIS 300, BIS 499 Detected section role: primary-requirement-section Schedulable: yes Reason: primary requirement cue Issue: none

[parser prerequisite filter audit] Owner id: uw-bothell-science-technology-and-society Source URL: https://www.uwb.edu/ias/undergraduate/majors/science-technology-society Section title: Yes! Many U.S. universities (not to mention a larger number of European ones) offer master’s degrees and doctorates in Science and Technology Studies or closely related fields like History and Philosophy of Science. But STS majors are also well equipped for graduate programs in the social sciences and humanities, including anthropology, sociology, history, and policy studies, as well as for law school and M.B.A. programs. Raw line: Yes! Many U.S. universities (not to mention a larger number of European ones) offer master’s degrees and doctorates in Science and Technology Studies or closely related fields like History and Philosophy of Science. But STS majors are also well equipped for graduate programs in the social sciences and humanities, including anthropology, sociology, history, and policy studies, as well as for law school and M.B.A. programs. requirementCueLines: The following degree requirements are required as of Autumn 2024 quarter. Students who entered the Science, Technology, & Society (STS) major prior to Autumn 2024 have different requirements. Please see your Academic Advisor for questions and academic planning.
B WRIT 133 or B WRIT 134 or ENGL 131 or equivalent (5 credits)
B WRIT 135 or ENGL 141 or equivalent (5 credits)
BISSTS 307 Science, Technology and Society (5 credits)
BISSTS 355 History of Science and Technology (5 credits)
BES 301 Science Methods and Practice (5 credits) – minimum 2.0 GPA required
BIS 312 Approaches to Social Research OR BIS 340 Approaches to Cultural Research (5 credits) – minimum 2.0 GPA required
Residency Requirement: 30 credits must be completed in residency at UW Bothell.

Interdisciplinary Practice & Reflection (IPR): The IPR requirement can overlap with the 70 credits of major coursework, or it can be completed through elective credits. Please see the IPR webpage for course options.

Upper Division Credit Policy: A maximum of 35 credits earned in 100- and 200-level courses may apply toward the major. Remaining credits must be earned in upper division courses (300-level and above).
BISSTS 355 History of Science and Technology
BES 301 Science Methods and Practice (min 2.0 grade)
B DATA 200 Introduction to Data Studies
B DATA 232 Introduction to Data Visualization
B MATH 215 Statistics for Health Sciences
BIS 302 Issues in Mathematics Across Cultures
BIS 304 Introduction to Political Economy and the Environment
BIS 319 Public Arts and Ecological Restoration
BIS 329 Topics in Mathematics Across the Curriculum
B PHYS 484 Physics, Society and Industry

Courses approved for this requirement apply the theories and/or methods of one or more disciplines in the social sciences and humanities to the study of science, technology, engineering, mathematics, or medicine. Approved courses may also explore how artistic practice can be informed by scientific concepts of technological forms.
BIS 458 Energy, the Environment, and Society
chooseStatements: STS majors take courses that examine science and technology from a broad range of perspectives. In Understanding Statistics (BIS 315) and Science Methods and Practice (BES 301), students learn to think like a scientist – to formulate research hypotheses, collect data, and reason with numbers. In Science, Technology, and Society (BISSTS 307), the core class in the STS major, students learn to think like a social scientist, and to approach science as a social, cultural, and political practice. For their remaining STS courses, students choose classes that examine how social, scientific, and technological factors intertwine in areas ranging from environmental restoration to global health.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Science, Technology & Society (BA) parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: The following degree requirements are required as of Autumn 2024 quarter. Students who entered the Science, Technology, & Society (STS) major prior to Autumn 2024 have different requirements. Please see your Academic Advisor for questions and academic planning.
B WRIT 133 or B WRIT 134 or ENGL 131 or equivalent (5 credits)
B WRIT 135 or ENGL 141 or equivalent (5 credits)
BISSTS 307 Science, Technology and Society (5 credits)
BISSTS 355 History of Science and Technology (5 credits)
BES 301 Science Methods and Practice (5 credits) – minimum 2.0 GPA required
BIS 312 Approaches to Social Research OR BIS 340 Approaches to Cultural Research (5 credits) – minimum 2.0 GPA required

parsedRequirementGroups.label: BES 301 or BIS 315 or BISSTS 307
parsedRequirementGroups.sourceHeading: STS majors take courses that examine science and technology from a broad range of perspectives. In Understanding Statistics (BIS 315) and Science Methods and Practice (BES 301), students learn to think like a scientist – to formulate research hypotheses, collect data, and reason with numbers. In Science, Technology, and Society (BISSTS 307), the core class in the STS major, students learn to think like a social scientist, and to approach science as a social, cultural, and political practice. For their remaining STS courses, students choose classes that examine how social, scientific, and technological factors intertwine in areas ranging from environmental restoration to global health.

parsedRequirementGroups.label: BIS 300 or BIS 499
parsedRequirementGroups.sourceHeading: STS is a major field of study within the Interdisciplinary Arts and Sciences (IAS) Program. STS majors primarily take courses offered through the IAS program, including Interdisciplinary Inquiry (BIS 300) and the Portfolio Capstone (BIS 499). S&T refers to the Science and Technology Program, which has its own majors (like Biology and Electrical Engineering) and an independent set of degree requirements. Students in the STS program may take S&T classes to fulfill General Elective and Natural World requirements – just as S&T students may take STS courses to fulfill some of the requirements for their majors.
